volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Braggart Soldier (Section 2.5.473 - Section 2.6.558)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Miles Gloriosus, or The Braggart Captain

sections.label: Section 2.5.473
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I fear it much . But no one shall ever make her to be any other than her own twin-sister .

sections.label: Section 2.5.473b
sections.original: Sed numquam quisquam faciet quin soror
sections.translation: ’ twas she , in troth , that you saw toying : and , in fact , ’ tis plain that it is she , as you remark .

sections.label: Section 2.5.475
sections.original: Id quidem palam est eam esse , ut dicis ; quid propius fuit ,
sections.translation: What was there more likely than that I should have been undone , if I had spoken of it to my master .

sections.label: Section 2.5.476b
sections.translation: Then , if you ’ re wise , you ’ ll hold your tongue . It befits a servant to know of more than he speaks . I ’ m going to leave you , that I may not at all participate in your designs . And I shall go to our neighbour here ; these turmoils of yours don ’ t please me .

sections.label: Section 2.5.480
sections.original: erus si veniet , si me quaeret , hic ero : hinc me arcessito . —
sections.translation: My master , if he comes , should he inquire for me , I shall be there ; send for me next door .

sections.label: Section 2.6.481
sections.original: Satin abiit ille neque erile negotium
sections.translation: Well , he ’ s off ; nor cares he any more for his master ’ s business than if he were not in his service . For sure she really is now here in-doors in the house , for I myself found her just now lying down in our house .

sections.label: Section 2.6.485
sections.original: certum est nunc observationi operam dare .
sections.translation: I am resolved now to employ myself in watching .

sections.label: Section 2.6.486
sections.original: non hercle hisce homines me marem , sed feminam
sections.translation: Faith , but these men here , these servants of my neighbour the Captain , take me not to be a man , but a woman , so much do they trifle with me . My lady guest , who came here yesterday from Athens with the gentleman , my guest ,

sections.label: Section 2.6.490
sections.original: tractatam et ludificatam , ingenuam et liberam ?
sections.translation: is she to be mauled about and made fun of here in the street — a lady , free-born and free ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.491
sections.original: Perii hercle , hic ad me recta habet rectam viam .
sections.translation: By my troth , I ’ m undone . He ’ s coming in a straight line up towards me . I fear that this matter may cause me great trouble , so far as I have heard this old gentleman speak .

sections.label: Section 2.6.494
sections.original: Accedam ad hominem . tun , Sceledre , hic , scelerum caput ,
sections.translation: I ’ ll up to this fellow . Was it you , Sceledrus , source of mischief , that were just now

sections.label: Section 2.6.495
sections.original: meam ludificavisti hospitam ante aedis modo ?
sections.translation: making fun of my lady guest before the house ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.496
sections.translation: Good neighbour , listen , I beg .

sections.label: Section 2.6.496b
sections.translation: I , listen to you ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.497
sections.translation: I wish to clear myself .

sections.label: Section 2.6.497b
sections.translation: You , clear yourself to me , who have done an action so gross and so unbecoming ? And because you are soldiers [*] , do you suppose ,

sections.label: Section 2.6.500
sections.translation: you gallows-bird , that you may do what you like with us ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.501
sections.translation: May I —?

sections.label: Section 2.6.501b
sections.original: At ita me di deaeque omnis ament ,
sections.translation: But so may all the Gods and Goddesses prosper me , if a punishment with the rod [*] is not given to you at my request , a long and lasting one , from morning to evening ; because you have been breaking my gutters and my tiles ,

sections.label: Section 2.6.505
sections.original: ibi dum condignam te sectatus essectatu's simiam ,
sections.translation: while you were following there a monkey like your own self [*] ; because , too , you have been peeping down from there at my guest in my house , when he was caressing and fondling his mistress ; besides , you have dared to accuse the chaste lady of your master of criminality , and myself of a heinous offence ;

sections.label: Section 2.6.510
sections.original: tum quod tractavisti hospitam ante aedis meam :
sections.translation: and further , because you have dared to maul about my lady guest before my house . If the punishment of the whip is not given to you , I will cause your master to be more laden with disgrace than the sea is full of waves in a heavy storm .

sections.label: Section 2.6.514
sections.original: Ita sum coactus , Periplectomene , ut nesciam
sections.translation: I am driven to such straits , Periplecomenus , that I don ’ t know

sections.label: Section 2.6.515
sections.original: utrum me postulare prius ⋯ tecum aequom siet ;
sections.translation: whether it is fitter for me rather to dispute this matter with you , or whether , if she is not our lady , and if our lady was not seen by me , it seems more proper for me to excuse myself to you ; as even now I don ’ t know which I saw , so like is that guest of yours to our lady —

sections.label: Section 2.6.520
sections.translation: if , indeed , she is not the same person .

sections.label: Section 2.6.520b
sections.original: Vise ad me intro , iam scies .
sections.translation: Go into my house and look : you ’ ll soon see .

sections.label: Section 2.6.521
sections.translation: May I go ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.521b
sections.original: Quin te iubeo ; et placide noscita .
sections.translation: Why , I command you ; go and examine at your leisure .

sections.label: Section 2.6.522
sections.translation: I am determined to do so .

sections.label: Section 2.6.522b
sections.translation: Ho ! Philocomasium [*] ! pass instantly , with all speed , into my house ; ’ tis absolutely necessary . Afterwards , when Sceledrus shall have come out from my house ,

sections.label: Section 2.6.525
sections.original: transcurrito ad vos rursum curriculo domum .
sections.translation: pass quickly , with all haste , back again to your own house . By my troth , now , I ’ m afraid she ’ ll be making some blunder . Should he not see the woman ⋯ My door opens .

sections.label: Section 2.6.528
sections.translation: O ye immortal Gods ! A woman more like ,

sections.label: Section 2.6.529_530
sections.original: magisque eándem , ut pote quae non sit eadem , non reor
sections.translation: and more the same , who is not the same , I do not think the Gods could make .

sections.label: Section 2.6.531b
sections.translation: What now ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.531c
sections.translation: I certainly merit chastisement .

sections.label: Section 2.6.532
sections.translation: What then ? Is it she ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.532b
sections.translation: Although ’ tis she , ’ tis not she .

sections.label: Section 2.6.533
sections.translation: Have you seen this lady ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.533b
sections.translation: I have seen both her and the gentleman , your guest , caressing and kissing .

sections.label: Section 2.6.534b
sections.translation: Is it she ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.534c
sections.translation: I know not .

sections.label: Section 2.6.535
sections.translation: Would you know for certain ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.535b
sections.translation: I should like to .

sections.label: Section 2.6.535c
sections.translation: Go you this instant into your own house : see whether your lady is within .

sections.label: Section 2.6.536b
sections.translation: Very well : you ’ ve advised me rightly . I ’ ll be out again to you this instant .

sections.label: Section 2.6.538
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I never saw any man more cleverly fooled , and by more singular devices .

sections.label: Section 2.6.540
sections.translation: But here he is coming .

sections.label: Section 2.6.540b
sections.translation: Periplecomenus , by Gods and men , and by my own folly , and by your knees ! I do beseech you —

sections.label: Section 2.6.542
sections.translation: What now ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.542b
sections.translation: Pardon my ignorance and my folly ; now , at length , I know that I am half-witted , blind , and thoughtless ;

sections.label: Section 2.6.545
sections.translation: for , behold ! Philocomasium is at home .

sections.label: Section 2.6.545b
sections.translation: How , then , hang-dog [*] . Have you seen them both ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.546b
sections.translation: I have seen them .

sections.label: Section 2.6.546c
sections.translation: I wish you to bring your master to me .

sections.label: Section 2.6.547
sections.original: Meruisse equidem me maxumum fateor malum ,
sections.translation: Indeed , I confess that I deserve a very great punishment ; and I own that I have done a wrong to your lady guest . But I thought that she was the lady of my master ,

sections.label: Section 2.6.550
sections.original: cui me custodem erus addidit miles meus .
sections.translation: to whom the Captain , my master , gave me as a keeper ; for it is not possible for water ever to be drawn more like to water from the same well , than is she to this lady guest of yours . And I will confess , as well , that I did look through the skylight into your house .

sections.label: Section 2.6.554b
sections.original: Quid ni fateare , ego quod viderim ?
sections.translation: Why shouldn ’ t you confess what I saw myself ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.555
sections.original: et ibi osculantem meum hospitem cum ista hospita
sections.translation: And there saw in your house this lady guest of yours , kissing .

sections.label: Section 2.6.556
sections.translation: You saw her ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.556b
sections.translation: I saw her . Why should I deny what I did see ? But I fancied that I had seen Philocomasium .

sections.label: Section 2.6.558
sections.original: Ratun istic me hominem esse omnium minimi preti ,
sections.translation: And did you suppose me to be the very vilest of all men , in allowing [*] , with my own knowledge ,
